title: echites umbellatus var. typicus
intro: Echites umbellatus var. typicus, commonly known as the Poison Nut, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Echites umbellatus var. typicus are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
